concepts.title: Properties of binomial expansion
concepts.conceptBody:
Properties of binomial expansion

(i) There are $\left( n+1 \right)$ terms in the expansion.

(ii) In each term, sum of the indices of ' $x$ ' and ' $y$ ' is equal to ' $n$ '.

(iii) In any term, the lower suffix of ' $C$ ' is equal to the index of ' $y$ ', and the index of $x=n-$ (lower suffix of C).

(iv) Because ${{~}^{\text{n}}}{{\text{C}}_{\text{r}}}={{~}^{\text{n}}}{{\text{C}}_{\text{n}-\text{r, }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}}$

so we have:

${{~}^{\text{n}}}{{\text{C}}_{0}}={{~}^{\text{n}}}{{\text{C}}_{\text{n}}}$

${{~}^{\text{n}}}{{\text{C}}_{1}}={{~}^{\text{n}}}{{\text{C}}_{\text{n}-1}}$

${{~}^{\text{n}}}{{\text{C}}_{2}}={{~}^{\text{n}}}{{\text{C}}_{\text{n}-2}}$ etc.

It follows that the coefficients of terms equidistant from the beginning and the ends are equal.

Examples 1 

(1) Simplify ${{\left( x+\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}-1} \right)}^{6}}+{{\left( x-\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}-1} \right)}^{6}}$ 

Solution : Let $\sqrt{\left( {{x}^{2}}-1 \right)}=a$, so we have :

$(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{a})^6+(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{a})^6$

$=\left[\mathrm{x}^6+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_1 \mathrm{x}^5 \cdot \mathrm{a}+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_2 \cdot \mathrm{x}^4 \cdot \mathrm{a}^2+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_3 \mathrm{x}^3 \mathrm{a}^3+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{x}^2 \mathrm{a}^4+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_5 \mathrm{x}^2 \mathrm{a}^4+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_5 \mathrm{xaa}^5+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{a}^6\right]$

$=\left[\mathrm{x}^6-{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_1 \mathrm{x}^5 \mathrm{a}+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_2 \cdot \mathrm{x}^4 \cdot \mathrm{a}^2-{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_3 \mathrm{x}^3 \mathrm{a}^3+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{x}^2 \mathrm{a}^4-{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_5 \mathrm{xa}^5+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_5 \mathrm{xa}^5+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{a}^6\right]$

$=2\left[\mathrm{x}^6+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{x}^4 \mathrm{a}^2+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{x}^2 \mathrm{a}^4+{ }^6 \mathrm{Ca}^6\right]$

$= 2\left[\mathrm{x}^6+15 \mathrm{x}^4\left(\mathrm{x}^2-1\right)+15 \mathrm{x}^2\left(\mathrm{x}^2-1\right)+\left(\mathrm{x}^2-1\right)^3\right]$

$=2\left[\mathrm{x}^6+15 \mathrm{x}^6-15 \mathrm{x}^4\left(\mathrm{x}^2-1\right)+15 \mathrm{x}^2\left(\mathrm{x}^2-1\right)+\left(\mathrm{x}^2-1\right)^3\right]$

$=2\left[\mathrm{x}^6+15 \mathrm{x}^6-15 \mathrm{x} 4+15 \mathrm{x}^6+15 \mathrm{x}^2-30 \mathrm{x}^4+\mathrm{x}^6-1-3 \mathrm{x}^4+3 \mathrm{x}^3\right]$

$=2\left[32 \mathrm{x}^6-48 \mathrm{x}^4+18 \mathrm{x}^2-1\right]$

Example 2 

In this expansion of ${{(x+a)}^{n}}$, if the sum of odd-terms be ' P ' and sum of even be ' Q ' Prove that: 

(i) ${{P}^{2}}-{{Q}^{2}}={{\left( {{x}^{2}}-{{a}^{2}} \right)}^{n}}$ 

(ii) $4PQ={{(x+a)}^{2n}}-{{(x-a)}^{2n}}$ 

Solution: 

$(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{a})^{\mathrm{n}}=\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}}+{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_1 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{a}+{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}-2} \mathrm{a}^2+{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_3{ }^{\mathrm{n}-3} \mathrm{a}^3+\cdots \ldots \ldots+\mathrm{nC}_{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{a}^{\mathrm{n}}$

$=\left(\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}}+{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}-2} \mathrm{a}^2+\cdots \cdots \cdots\right)+\left({ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_1 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{nC}_3 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}-3} \mathrm{a}^3+\cdots \ldots \ldots \ldots\right)$

$(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{a})^{\mathrm{n}}=\mathrm{P}+\mathrm{Q}$………………… (1)

$\text { and }(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{a})^{\mathrm{n}}=\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}}-{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_1 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{a}+{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}-2} \mathrm{a}^2-{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_3 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}-3} \mathrm{a}^3+\cdots \ldots+(-1)^{\mathrm{n} n} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{a}^{\mathrm{n}}$

$=\left(\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}}+{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}-2} \mathrm{a}^2+\cdots \cdots \cdots\right)-\left({ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_1 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{a}+{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_3 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}-3} \mathrm{a}^3+\right.$……)

$(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{a})^{\mathrm{n}}=\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{Q}$………………… (2)

Now we have:

(1) ${{\text{P}}^{2}}-{{\text{Q}}^{2}}$ $~=\left( \text{P}+\text{Q} \right)\left( \text{P}-\text{Q} \right)$

$~={{(\text{x}+\text{a})}^{\text{n}}}{{(\text{x}-\text{a})}^{\text{n}}}$

$~={{\text{P}}^{2}}-{{\text{Q}}^{2}}={{\left( {{\text{x}}^{2}}-{{\text{a}}^{2}} \right)}^{\text{n}}}$

(2) $4\text{PQ}$ $~={{(\text{P}+\text{Q})}^{2}}-{{(\text{P}-\text{Q})}^{2}}$

$~={{(\text{x}+\text{a})}^{2\text{n}}}-{{(\text{x}-\text{a})}^{2\text{n}}}$

Example 3 

Prove that ${{(101)}^{50}}>{{(100)}^{50}}+{{(99)}^{50}}$ 

Solution: 

${{(101)}^{50}}$ $~={{(100+1)}^{50}}$

$~={{(100)}^{50}}+{{~}^{50}}{{\text{C}}_{1}}{{(100)}^{49}}+{{~}^{50}}{{\text{C}}_{2}}{{(100)}^{48}}+\ldots \ldots \ldots \ldots +1$…….. (i)

${{(99)}^{50}}$ $~={{(100-1)}^{50}}$

$~={{(100)}^{50}}-{{~}^{50}}{{\text{c}}_{1}}{{(100)}^{49}}+{{~}^{50}}{{\text{c}}_{2}}{{(100)}^{48}}-\ldots \ldots \ldots \ldots +1$……….. (ii)

eq. (i) - eq. (ii):

${{(101)}^{50}}-$ $~{{(99)}^{50}}=2\left[ {{~}^{50}}{{\text{C}}_{1}}{{(100)}^{49}}+{{~}^{50}}{{\text{C}}_{3}}{{(100)}^{47}}+\ldots \ldots \ldots ... \right.$]

$~=2\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left( 50!/1!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }49! \right){{(100)}^{49}}+2.{{~}^{50}}{{\text{C}}_{3}}{{(100)}^{47}}+$…………

$~=100\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{(100)}^{49}}+\left( \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A }\!\!~\!\!\text{ positive }\!\!~\!\!\text{ number }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } \right)$

$~={{(100)}^{50}}+\left( \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A }\!\!~\!\!\text{ positive }\!\!~\!\!\text{ number }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } \right)$

$~{{(101)}^{50}}-{{(99)}^{50}}>{{(100)}^{50}}$

Or $~{{(101)}^{50}}>{{(101)}^{50}}+{{(99)}^{50}}$
